pages.raw_text:
That the fight to regain the use
OF the streets for speaking pur-
poses will reopen tomorrow, 10 the
announcement made In the issue
Of the Industrial Worker Of Febru-
ary rs. For circulating copies OF
the paper, two members OF tho
I. w. w. organization were arrest
ed this morning by Patrolman Ful.
Ter.


Charles DevIin, the second Of the
duet to be apprehended, announced
at the police station that there ara
over 1000 !. w. w. men In Spokane
today, and that the number 10 orov-.
ing rapidly with each incoming
train. Z VI


A committee Of three called on
Mayor Pratt at the city hall this
morning. Although none would
talk about their errand. 1t was un-s
derstood from. reliable source
that their mission was made, for
the purpose OF trying to persuade
the mayor to instruct the police
to not molest the street speakers
when the fight is renewed.


THE CHIEFS REMARK


Chief Sullivan refused to make
any statement OF what will be done
in case the fight opens up afresh
tomorrow. "Greet the devil when
he comes," was all the chief would
say. He was asked II the same
tactics would be pursued by the po-
lice that characterized their side
of the recent trouble and he re-
piled, Il presume So"


The offices OF the l. w, w were
raided recently In both Cocur
d'alene and Hillyard and the pro-
prietors of the places arrested In
both cases. This was done accord
ing to Captain OF Detectives Burns,
for the purpose of warding off A.
much trouble as possible by weak
ening the forces Of tho "conspira-
tors" ,s


ARRESTS GETTING FREQUENT


IF. McCarty was the first OF tho
pair arrested for circulating the
official paper. Of the industrial
workers, this morning. He wan
brought to the station by Patrols
man Fuller at 10: 40. Half an hour
later Charles Devlin, another Of tho
same brigade was ''pinched" on the
same charge. Both mon were
booked with vaurancy and both re-
fused 10 elueidate ON the reputed
plans OF tho orsinaxltion IN the
future,
